text: Or possibly ‘on his own hand’, as if branding himself with the mark of the divine service.
The text of this verse seems confused, and it seems possible that the heathen gods are once more being challenged to produce proofs of successful prophecies; cf. 42.9; 43.9 above.
Literally, ‘if there is a lie at his right hand’; it was the right side that was exposed in ancient warfare.
